frameworks.name: residual dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Disentangles recurrent dynamical contributions from inputs and feedforward processing in population trajectories.

frameworks.name: line-attractor dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Integration-like regime recoverable from residual dynamics in decision-making areas.

frameworks.name: rotational dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Alternative recurrent regime distinguished by residual dynamics in cortical populations.

frameworks.name: recurrent neural network model
frameworks.one_line: Two-area RNN simulations used as ground-truth validation.

core_question: How can recurrent contributions to neural population dynamics be separated from input-driven and feedforward processing?
key_assumptions: Population trajectories are a deterministic dynamical system driven by external inputs
Removing input-driven components leaves state-dependent recurrent evolution as residual
manipulations: Brain area (LIP vs FEF vs PFC)
Ground-truth dynamical regime in two-area RNN simulations
key_findings: Residual dynamics reveal distinct recurrent computations across areas (integration, rotation, feedforward)
Method correctly recovers ground-truth recurrent contributions in RNN simulations
Line-attractor vs rotational regimes are distinguishable from residual dynamics
interpretations: Residual dynamics provides a principled way to attribute neural computation to recurrent vs feedforward sources
Framework extends to compare experimental data against alternative dynamical model hypotheses
